answer:
The correct answer is **\boxed{C}**.

The sampling time \( T_s = \frac{1}{f_s} \) is determined by the Nyquist rate, which requires \( f_s = 2 \times \text{bandwidth} = 10 \text{ kHz} \), giving \( T_s = 100 \mu s = 1 \times 10^{-4} \text{ sec} \). However, since **2000 signals** are multiplexed, the **total sampling period** becomes \( T_s = 2000 \times T_{\text{Nyquist}} = 200 \mu s = 2 \times 10^{-4} \text{ sec} \). The maximum pulse duration \( \tau_{\text{max}} \) is half the sampling interval (\( T_s/2 \)), yielding \( \tau_{\text{max}} = 100 \mu s
